sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Reimagining NASA.gov to tell Earth’s most important stories
sessions.description: NASA’s digital universe just got a major upgrade. With a bold new web experience and the Horizon Design System, NASA is now equipped to tell its most important stories—breakthroughs, missions, and cosmic wonders—with clarity, cohesion, and style. In this session, Ben and Megan pull back the curtain on designing for rocket scientists, sharing how vision meets velocity in a high-stakes collaboration. Expect real talk on aligning stakeholders, designing for scale, and building a system that inspires the world—one pixel at a time.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: upskill on skills and agentic workflows
sessions.description: Skills are how AI learns a domain: your research frameworks, handoff standards, and definitions of done, so every session starts informed, not from scratch. This session goes beyond the basics to show how to design, author, and deploy skills that make agents measurably faster and more precise for the work at hand. You’ll leave with a skill you helped build, not just a framework to complete later.

sessions.title: Closing keynote
sessions.description: André Anjos is a musician, not a designer, but he believes everyone is facing the same shift. Average has become free. Machines can now produce competent, good-enough work instantly, whether it’s a song or a screen. That means the only work worth doing is the work only you can make. After twenty years in music, he has learned one enduring truth: the work you create for yourself is the work that ultimately reaches everyone else. There has never been a better time to have a voice.

sessions.title: Build like it's 2030. Listen like you mean it.
sessions.description: The same shift that democratized research five years ago is now coming for design. Researchers didn't disappear—they got more strategic, defined what good looked like, and built the systems that let everyone else ship to their bar. AI replaces UI production, but judgment, taste, and customer understanding become what wins.

sessions.title: The edge case is everyone
sessions.description: T-Mobile set out to rethink that experience. Willie King, Business Development Manager at T-Mobile, and Lex Liley, Associate Director of Product Strategy at Kettle, will share how their teams partnered with the DHH community to challenge assumptions about voice communication.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Dimensional shift: sculpting in Figma Draw
sessions.description: In this session, Josh demonstrates his unified Figma Draw workflow, sharing practical techniques for using 2D vector effects to create 3D depth. Discover how becoming a student again makes your brain 'porous' to new dimensions, bridging the gap between flat design and 3D. Learn why picking up a new tool isn't just a technical upgrade, it’s a creative lifeline.

sessions.title: Own the loop: how designers and researchers win with AI evals
sessions.description: AI is everywhere, but how do we know if it's actually good? The teams winning with AI aren't just moving fast, they're measuring well. And that measurement starts with a human-centered definition of "good" that lives in design and research. AI evals have long been an engineering problem. That's changing. As capabilities commoditize, design is the new moat. This talk is an invitation for designers and researchers to lead and become architects of how we evaluate AI against real human needs.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Managing multiple creative passions in a world that tells you to specialize
sessions.description: Lauren will share how she’s managed multiple creative passions, throughout her 15-year career, in a world that tells you to specialize.

sessions.title: Prompts to fashion: AI-powered 3D-printed garments
sessions.description: This session brings that vision to the Config stage through a live reveal of 3 looks designed with Weavy and printed using this new workflow, showing the design community what zero-waste, on-demand, fully digitized fashion actually looks and feels like. Danit is making clear that the tools to build a circular, on-demand fashion future already exist today.

sessions.title: The canvas is not dead
sessions.description: You've probably heard it: 'the canvas is dead. Just prompt your way to a UI and ship it.' But Catt doesn't work on simple problems. She designs for tens of millions of users, inside products with years of context and use cases AI tools can't easily handle. When the complexity is that deep, you can't skip the part where you actually think. She'll walk through her framework for deciding what deserves deep, by-hand exploration versus what to hand off to the machine. You'll leave knowing how to match your tools to your actual problems.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: In praise of dark mode
sessions.description: Good design emits as little light as possible. This talk makes the case for Dark Mode Maximalism — that all user interfaces should be designed in dark mode. Drawing on two decades of design/engineering practice and arcane scholarship, Baytaş explains that dark mode is not a toggle, but the default condition of good design.

sessions.title: Writing for humans in an AI world
sessions.description: Content designers at Anthropic don't write Claude's responses—they write everything around them: prompt starters, UI copy, feature names, voice principles. This talk explores how content design shifts when your interface understands natural language and how to teach collaboration (not just use) through the edges of the experience. Whether you're designing AI products or wondering how AI changes the craft, this talk addresses the challenge of designing frames around intelligence that talks back.

sessions.title: Designing in the age of the 10x engineer
sessions.description: Andy Zhang will explore how the designer's role evolves when creation becomes frictionless. He’ll look at the future of dynamic, intent-driven experiences and discuss the questions we must ask to adapt our product processes—ensuring that faster engineering leads to better, human-centered products rather than just more features.

sessions.title: Don't forget the feeling: a critique of software in 2026
sessions.description: What does making feel like in 2026? Luis will explore how he learned to stop worrying and love AI, or at least stop rejecting it to accelerate his creative output. Through critique of popular AI tools, Luis will explain that in this fast paced environment it's essential that we don't forget that feeling and brand are what brings users back.

sessions.title: How to build content systems for AI product workflows
sessions.description: With AI, teams are shipping faster than ever. Designers are prototyping directly in the product. Engineers are coding at the speed of thought. But every tool your team touches is generating copy. Keeping it consistent, on-brand, and compliant—has never been harder. In this workshop, Jessica and Jolena, co-founders of Ditto, will show you how to build a content system that keeps up. Using Figma Make and Ditto, you’ll wire together a living system that generates copy from your guidelines, flags what doesn’t align, and gives every teammate—and every AI tool—the same source of copy truth.

sessions.title: Human methods, new material: designing with AI for the classroom
sessions.description: How do you build with AI for a world as human, messy, and high-stakes as the classroom? This talk takes you inside the 0–1 journey of Goodnotes Classroom: from real teacher workarounds and classroom observations to fast prototypes, co-creation sessions, and product decisions shaped by educators themselves. We’ll share how human methods became the foundation for designing with AI as a new material, and why the best way to build faster may be to get closer to the people you’re building for.

sessions.title: Usernames on Instagram: how a craft fix became an identity crisis
sessions.description: This talk traces that spiral—from @dril to Baudrillard, from the pumpkin spice latte to the phone icon that still looks like a landline—and arrives somewhere concrete: In the Internet Age, users don't just adapt to what we build, they create something new with it. Representations drift from reality, and people don’t just adapt to products—they complete them. The result is a firsthand case study in product judgment when human behavior becomes the strongest constraint, why designing with curiosity and far-fetched futurism matters even more in fast-moving environments.

sessions.title: Skill issue: how we built an OS for PMs at Figma
sessions.description: This is the story of how a small group of volunteers, empowered by a culture of experimentation, created an AI tool that quickly became part of every PM's daily workflow. This talk is for product managers, designers, and anyone interested in shaping the tools that shape work itself.

sessions.title: ESPN's migration playbook: the move to Figma with One North
sessions.description: Each game day broadcast features a host of slick visuals – digital scoreboards, player stats, and matchup graphics. But behind the scenes, each individual design requires a huge lift to get from concept to live on air. In this session, One North and ESPN will share how migrating from Photoshop to Figma revealed new ways to collaborate, scale, and accelerate workflows. From initial adoption to transformative impact, this talk highlights not only how Figma helped push ESPN's design system to the next level, but how their design system pushed One North to up our game, too.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: unlocking speed and visual expression with [REDACTED]
sessions.description: Learn about new ways to bring visual expression, consistency, and speed into your design workflow with [REDACTED].

sessions.title: Dense by design
sessions.description: Why did Google beat Yahoo? Why does Bloomberg Terminal still dominate? It's not about cramming more onto the screen—it's about maximizing value in time and space. This talk unpacks UI density from first principles, moving through Tufte, gestalt psychology, and perceived performance to arrive at a framework you can actually use.

sessions.title: The metacognitive design loop
sessions.description: Learn how Epsilon's Product Design Team transformed front-end development by shifting the QA process to the very beginning of the design phase. By combining a Metacognitive Design Loop with Figma's Code Connect and MCP with their CORE UI component library, the team has revolutionized product delivery and achieved unprecedented development velocity—moving from design to production faster than ever before.

sessions.title: Opening keynotes
sessions.description: This talk explores human computer interaction through the design of programmable creative tools. Drawing on our work maintaining Strudel, a live-coded music system, we’ll examine how having absolute freedom over text interfaces enables more intimate and natural interactions. With a background in instrument making, we frame software as an interactive artifact meant to be tuned and learned through use engaging with a design space that hasn’t fully crystallized yet, where shaping your own environment becomes a playground for creativity.

sessions.title: 10 years of daily sketching
sessions.description: Ten years ago, after a rough year, Zach Lieberman posted a small code sketch on Instagram on New Year's Day and he's posted one every day since. This talk is about what he's learned from that practice of making something every day. He'll focus on the importance of boredom, how to structure iteration, how he structures research and most importantly, finding creative freedom.

sessions.title: The marathon designer: creative endurance beyond prompts and pixels
sessions.description: In a world obsessed with speed & chaos, design’s biggest threat isn’t AI—it’s burnout, pixel-pushing, doing things the old way, waiting for permission. This talk introduces a framework for creative endurance beyond prompts and pixels. Through three core practices—sustainable pacing, builder culture, and versatile hiring—Socrates Charisis shares lessons from running a bootstrapped studio in Athens. Expect real stories, actionable systems, and new ways to keep creativity alive when the work gets dull and the tools keep changing.

sessions.title: Closing happy hour
sessions.description: Toast to new connections and inspirational sessions at Config Commons!

sessions.title: The future is low-tech: lessons from the early 2000s
sessions.description: We’ll rewind to the early 2000s—when tech was low-tech, playful and non-extractive. Think: loading bars, Tamagotchis and RollerCoaster Tycoon in a cereal box. This talk explores calm, nervous-system-friendly design: attention regulation, slowness as a feature, autonomy-first mechanics, real-world interaction and gentle feedback loops. A call for designers and builders to make tech feel human again.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Opening keynote
sessions.description: Design and product-building are changing, and so are the roles we play in the process. This means rethinking product development from the ground up—shifting mindsets and methods to make great work. Figma’s biggest product launch of the year kicks off Config.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive: inside the Weave workflow
sessions.description: Dive into how Weave creative work actually gets made—not the polished final frame, but the decisions behind it. Explore branching, sequencing, iteration, model chaining, edits, mistakes, recoveries, and the systems thinking that turn AI from a magic trick into a real creative process.

sessions.title: Quirk up your workplace
sessions.description: This talk is about encouraging people to be a little more silly, a little more playful when you’re at work. The rituals, gimmicks, and stunts we’ve done at GitHub to make remote work fun and interesting.

sessions.title: Be the director: how stage craft informs product craft
sessions.description: The screen is the stage, and the user is the audience. In this talk, we’ll explore how to think like a stage director by making intentional choices, defining the non-negotiables in your script, and finding your product’s spine. And why the show must go on.

sessions.title: Scooter, screen and duct-tape: from hack to habit
sessions.description: What if your next great design tool doesn’t exist yet? At Ather Energy, designers built their own using Arduinos, iPads, and Figma. This talk unpacks the ways of going outside the design loop, how hacks become habits, and how scrappy experimentation helps teams validate ideas, influence stakeholders, and build magical real-world experiences faster.

sessions.title: Figma meets hardware: prototyping for the physical world

speakers.name: Sam Thorne
speakers.title: Senior Principal Designer for Creative Technology

sessions.description: What if Figma’s canvas could extend into the physical world? At Electrolux, we use Figma to design the next generation of home appliances. This talk shares how we bridged the digital-physical gap by building "Figma Displays" with off-the-shelf electronics to live-stream prototypes onto production-accurate screens. Learn how this new process shrunk our feedback loop from six months to just two minutes, completely revamping our workflow. You'll leave inspired to find your own ways to connect the digital canvas to the world around you.

sessions.title: The hidden craft of interface design in modern film
sessions.description: Step behind the screens of modern cinema. Jayse Hansen takes you from sketchbook to IMAX, revealing the story-driven way holograms, HUDs, and futuristic interface systems are designed, animated, and brought to life for blockbuster films. Spanning more than 20 major franchises, from Iron Man and The Hunger Games to Tron and Star Wars, this is a rare backstage look at the meticulously detailed craft of designing technology that doesn’t exist, but feels like it should.

sessions.title: Keynote
sessions.description: Holly Herndon has spent the last decade building at the frontier of music and AI, from the albums Platform and PROTO to Holly+, the first artist-controlled voice model, to the data consent tools behind Spawning. This talk covers her approach to making art with AI, what she calls Protocol Art: a process-based practice of designing the rule set that creates the conditions for art to be made. Holly will discuss how the model-making process becomes a site of authorship, and where her studio with Mat Dryhurst is heading next: orchestrating agents while remaining at the conceptual center.

sessions.title: Beyond design systems: designing for robots and seniors
sessions.description: While AI reshapes the world, many who need it most are left behind. At Norbert Health, Anna Oh designs robotic AI for seniors with dementia and limited mobility in nursing homes. Working directly with real patients and nurses, Oh and her team built human systems that help robots listen, respond, and comfort.

sessions.title: Everyone's still eating: building Notion's design system
sessions.description: Introducing a design system at a design-led company can be like trying to whip a tablecloth off a beautifully set table while people are still eating—and maybe put a nicer tablecloth on at the same time. As part of Notion’s newly formed design system team (est. 2025), we’ll share the tools and techniques we’re using to refactor Notion’s visual foundations and improve things from the inside, without slowing down our peers or compromising the spirit of thoughtful, handcrafted care that makes Notion feel like Notion.

sessions.title: From handoffs to upstream
sessions.description: A year ago, EDL made a bet. Not on a tool or a trend, but on a person and a different idea of what a design studio could own. Today, EDL pushes to clients' Git repos, runs their design systems in code, and sits upstream of their engineering teams. This wasn't accidental. It was intentional. This is the full story: the moment the team saw the opportunity, the rituals that made it real, the metrics that proved it worked, and what it means for any design leader wondering if their team could do the same.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: express yourself with Figma Draw
sessions.description: A hands-on collaborative session where attendees come together to create a shared art piece. This session celebrates the magic of expressive design and showcases what’s possible with Figma Draw.

sessions.title: Designing money: how to build a trustworthy brand in the age of AI
sessions.description: How do we pair machine learning with true artistry? What’s moral, what’s safe, and what genuinely resonates in this new creative world? These are the questions that confronted our team at Plaid as we sought to reshape the way money looks and feels in the age of AI. In this far-reaching talk, we’ll explore the ways authenticity has shaped the history of finance through the lens of graphic design and branding—and what machine learning means for its future.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: bringing designs to life with [REDACTED]
sessions.description: Ease into [REDACTED] in this session; we’ll show how to bring more energy into your product experience. Everything you need to know to get started with these new features.

sessions.title: From concept to confidence with Dscout AI
sessions.description: AI has accelerated the time between idea and execution. Teams can now generate concepts, prototypes, and products in days. Everyone’s a builder now. But speed without care, validation, and taste creates expensive mistakes. In this session, you’ll learn how Dscout’s AI-powered research suite supports the entire product development system—from ideation to iterative testing—while enabling designers, PMs, and product teams to make decisions quickly and confidently.

sessions.title: Designing the product that can do anything
sessions.description: Software, robotics, machine intelligence, and spatial computing have collided to present us with one of the greatest UX challenges of our time: How do you design an interface for a humanoid robot that can theoretically do anything? This talk will dive into the design challenges of teaching a robot about the world in three parts: Training robots to develop skills, building application interfaces to author task behaviors, and integrating with facilities to monitor robots’ effectiveness and realize the vision of the software-defined factory.

sessions.title: Design systems anarchy
sessions.description: Most people don't get into design systems to be a cop. But we've become exactly that. Product teams go around us while we create rules nobody follows. Punk figured this out decades ago. You need shared ethos, basic infrastructure, and people who care. Quality comes from culture, not compliance. Drawing from years running an indie record label and nearly 10 years leading systems at scale at Block, Dropbox, and Expedia, this talk applies punk's blueprint to systems. AI handed designers the means of production. The gatekeeping model is dead. Stop managing systems. Start nurturing subcultures.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Designing brand intelligence: from rules to expression
sessions.description: In an AI-powered design world, the designer’s role is fundamentally shifting. The real edge is no longer technical execution but how you think, decide, and design in a world changing faster than your design system. This talk reimagines brand guides as cognitive frameworks inspired by J Dilla, who reimagined rhythm and broke away from rigid quantization to create something more human, more fluid, more felt.

sessions.title: Legibility by design
sessions.description: Building trust in AI isn't about transparency; it’s about communication design. Whether it’s an autonomous vehicle or a generative agent, the challenge is the same: how much should a system communicate? Drawing from years at Waymo, this session explores a framework for trust. We’ll move beyond automation toward legibility, where users understand intent rather than just monitoring tasks. Learn to calibrate feedback to avoid alert fatigue and build AI products that feel predictable and fundamentally trustworthy.

sessions.title: AI will upend your process—for the better
sessions.description: AI gives us an opportunity to rethink how work gets done across the software delivery lifecycle. In this talk, I’ll show concrete examples of how AI removes bottlenecks that keep us from discovering and delivering value. We’ll explore how AI supports building design systems, generates implementation‑ready user stories, and writes production code directly from Figma assets. The result: faster validation, earlier feedback, and more time for craft.

sessions.title: The most important component: community
sessions.description: The more AI enters the creative process, the louder the question gets: what makes our work distinctly ours? This talk makes the case that community - real human connection, shared perspective, and creative kinship - is the answer. And why Figma is building tools that keep it at the center.

sessions.title: Prototypes, failures, and unexpected breakthroughs
sessions.description: Noah spends many days at Figma prototyping janky interactions, and other days perfecting editor microinteractions so subtle they almost go unnoticed. How do these two engineering mindsets interact? And how have they shaped the way Noah builds experiences like Figma Slides, FigPals, and interactive transit personality tests?

sessions.title: Gnarly by design: building systems with grit
sessions.description: Clean design systems are hard to create, but gritty, texture-heavy design systems are even harder. Join Beau for a journey into a video game design system and learn how to add some character to your squeaky-clean system and make it scaleable.

sessions.title: Designed to be read: making a system AI can actually use
sessions.description: Hear the story of how Meta worked directly with Figma to test design systems in Figma Make. You’ll learn how, in our quest to push the boundaries of AI-driven quality, Meta’s business design system team was inspired to rebuild their legacy design system for business tools from the ground up. Our talk covers the “sloppy” challenges we encountered, how we resolved them in close partnership with Figma, and outlines a 3-step repeatable playbook that any team can use to deliver high-quality AI-generated designs—even without rebuilding your entire design system.

sessions.title: How structured thinking gives your AI superpowers
sessions.description: The best designers in the AI era will use it to scale their thinking, not just generate stuff. This talk looks at how a more structured design process (in docs, issues, trackers, and briefs) turns AI from a one-off creative aid into a genuine collaborator.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: design to development workflows
sessions.description: A beginner-friendly guide to moving from design to code using Figma’s MCP server. Learn best practices for bringing code context into Figma with variable prefixes and Code Connect, and how to get stronger results from your design system.

sessions.title: The invisible mechanisms in your interfaces
sessions.description: For 15 years, Johannes has been taking interfaces apart and collecting the mechanisms underneath. There’s a name for when your cursor sits between bold and regular and the system can’t tell you which wins. It’s called cursor affinity. Your tools are full of mechanisms like this — each with an inventor, a constraint, and a lineage you can trace. This talk takes them apart, names them, and hands you a practice for disassembling any interface you touch. You’ll leave with the words to make others see what you see.

sessions.title: Preparing Rocket for the AI era
sessions.description: While many companies are still preparing for AI, Rocket is already embedding it into the fabric of how thousands of team members work every day. Learn how we're leveraging Figma's ecosystem—design systems, Code Connect, Figma Make, and the Figma MCP—to power AI-native workflows at scale and help more people achieve their dream of homeownership.

sessions.title: Badge pickup
sessions.description: Beat the rush! Grab your Config badge at Moscone North between 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: workflow tips—from slow to flow
sessions.description: In this session we’ll share a collection of helpful tips and workflow tricks that can help you work smarter, and faster on the canvas and beyond.

sessions.title: The world of street art and care-filled design
sessions.description: After studying art and design at UC Berkeley, Ella Rochelle-Lawton ran off to join the circus and designed a billboard about it. Since then, teaching street art history and spray paint has taught her that the best design lives on the street, where people can touch it and make it their own. In this talk, she'll share how working with teams from Adobe, Google, and Figma transformed her view of design from pixels to people. She’ll explore how street art, from the local legends to international artists, build belonging, redefine public space, and inspire community throughout the design process.

sessions.title: Design for the future of apps
sessions.description: Generative AI is not only about text chats and image generation. At Google, AI is also increasingly used to compose ephemeral UI on demand, a pattern called Generative UI. While this promises unparalleled personalization for each user, it also risks regressions in UX pillars like consistency and clear information hierarchy. Learn how designers can adapt design systems to realize the full potential GenUI while also mitigating its risks.

sessions.title: Your best practices are trapped in your head—and your AI has no idea
sessions.description: Your tone of voice, your component principles, your definition of "done"—this is the taste you've built over years. Right now it lives in your head, in scattered docs, or in places your tools can't see. Every AI session starts from scratch because that context is invisible. This talk shows how to connect those dots: building a shared knowledge layer so AI can help with specs, prototypes, and design systems—without you repeating yourself every time.

sessions.title: Building real-time user insights into your design workflow
sessions.description: Most design teams ship, then learn. You don't have to do that anymore. In this session, HeyMarvin CEO Prayag Narula walks through what it takes to build real-time user feedback into your designs. You'll hear about the integrations and tools that make it work in practice and what it looks like when research finally shows up where decisions get made. If you've ever shipped something and wished you'd heard from a user two weeks earlier, this one's for you.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Ready for the world
sessions.description: Vicki Tan is a product designer who stumbled into authorship through Figma—a tool that gave her the courage to create an illustrated book about decision-making. This talk reveals the unexpected, messy journey of turning a movable concept into print, exploring thousands of design iterations, learning new skills in writing and drawing, and how that led to an untraditional memoir. By the end, she hopes you’ll be ready for the world, and your own project, knowing that belief in your tools and yourself is all you need to make it happen.

sessions.title: 300GB story: how to inspire action when data isn’t enough
sessions.description: What if 300 GB of climate data could make you feel something? In this talk, two Harvard Design Engineering students share how they turned overwhelming datasets into Habitat2100, an interactive storytelling experience built in Figma that transforms data into empathy, urgency, and a call to act for our planet’s future.

sessions.title: Hacking AI for creative operations at scale
sessions.description: Most creative teams are using AI to make assets faster. The real opportunity is further upstream—inside the systems, workflows, and decision logic that govern how creative gets made at scale. In this session, Rory Flynn shows how mastering three core skills—deconstruction, system prompts, and workflow building—unlocks the full power of Figma Weave for any team. You'll see what real deployment looks like from inside a billion-dollar global consumer tech brand, and what the future of creative ops looks like when AI is built in from the start.

sessions.title: Closing keynote
sessions.description: Want to animate like the pros and spend less time wrestling the graph editor? This session reveals a capture-to-curve workflow that turns recorded gestures into clean, editable timing you can drop into the tools you use. We will map a motion in the room to a UI element live and unpack a practical loop: capture, curve, polish, name. You will leave with a shared motion language you can reuse across design and code, plus techniques to make interfaces feel natural so newcomers move with confidence and experts move faster. We will demystify the curve editor and show how to ship feel, not graphs.

sessions.title: Out of sync, on purpose: desktop design after mobile won
sessions.description: This talk is about designing for Spotify’s desktop app—the original Spotify experience that continues to evolve alongside mobile. It explores how a small, focused team interprets features not by copying them 1:1, but by tailoring execution to the platform’s strengths. Desktop plays a distinct role in people’s routines—a companion for deep focus, creation, and immersive listening. Through real examples, Giulio shows how being small, deliberate, and a little out of sync can transform consistency into craft.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: the many ways to make with Figma Make
sessions.description: Learn about all of the different ways to build with Figma Make, whether you’re creating a new prototype from scratch or starting from an existing codebase. We’ll cover guidelines & skills, how to provide great context, and some best practices for working with your design system.

sessions.title: From kickoff to canvas: connecting Google Workspace and Figma
sessions.description: Stop wasting time hunting for project context across scattered tabs. This session explores the connected surface between Google Workspace and Figma. See a high-productivity workflow in action, from live ideation in Meet to using Ask Gemini in Chat to instantly surface files, wrap up comment threads, and remove the administrative friction from your day.

sessions.title: Config Commons
sessions.description: Join us right outside of Moscone as we take over the block for a community hangout. Grab your conference badge, pick out your swag, and kick off your Config experience in a relaxed environment designed for meaningful conversations and spontaneous collaboration.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: build your first Weave workflow
sessions.description: Node-based tools look technical until you build something with them. In this hands-on session, you'll go from blank canvas to working workflow, connecting models, directing outputs, and seeing exactly how Figma Weave gives you control over every step.

sessions.title: Designing with confidence in an AI-powered world
sessions.description: AI has fundamentally changed the pace of design. Ideas move faster. Iterations are instant. Options are endless. But speed introduces a new tension: decisions that are harder to defend. In this session, Bobby Meixner shares findings from UserTesting’s latest research on defensible design in the age of AI and explores how leading teams are closing the confidence gap. You’ll see how embedding real human insight directly inside the design workflow allows teams to move quickly, without sacrificing certainty. Because in an AI-powered world, the real advantage isn’t speed. It’s conviction.

speakers.name: Sam Thorne
speakers.title: Senior Principal Designer for Creative Technology

sessions.title: Badge pickup
sessions.description: Beat the rush! Grab your Config badge at Moscone North between 9:00 AM - 7:00 PM.

sessions.title: Config Commons
sessions.description: Join us right outside of Moscone as we take over the block for a community hangout. Grab your conference badge, pick out your swag, and kick off your Config experience in a relaxed environment designed for meaningful conversations and spontaneous collaboration.

sessions.title: Reimagining NASA.gov to tell Earth’s most important stories
sessions.description: NASA’s digital universe just got a major upgrade. With a bold new web experience and the Horizon Design System, NASA is now equipped to tell its most important stories—breakthroughs, missions, and cosmic wonders—with clarity, cohesion, and style. In this session, Ben and Megan pull back the curtain on designing for rocket scientists, sharing how vision meets velocity in a high-stakes collaboration. Expect real talk on aligning stakeholders, designing for scale, and building a system that inspires the world—one pixel at a time.

sessions.title: Build like it's 2030. Listen like you mean it.
sessions.description: The same shift that democratized research five years ago is now coming for design. Researchers didn't disappear—they got more strategic, defined what good looked like, and built the systems that let everyone else ship to their bar. AI replaces UI production, but judgment, taste, and customer understanding become what wins.

sessions.title: The edge case is everyone
sessions.description: T-Mobile set out to rethink that experience. Willie King, Business Development Manager at T-Mobile, and Lex Liley, Associate Director of Product Strategy at Kettle, will share how their teams partnered with the DHH community to challenge assumptions about voice communication.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Dimensional shift: sculpting in Figma Draw
sessions.description: In this session, Josh demonstrates his unified Figma Draw workflow, sharing practical techniques for using 2D vector effects to create 3D depth. Discover how becoming a student again makes your brain 'porous' to new dimensions, bridging the gap between flat design and 3D. Learn why picking up a new tool isn't just a technical upgrade, it’s a creative lifeline.

sessions.title: Prompts to fashion: AI-powered 3D-printed garments
sessions.description: This session brings that vision to the Config stage through a live reveal of 3 looks designed with Weavy and printed using this new workflow, showing the design community what zero-waste, on-demand, fully digitized fashion actually looks and feels like. Danit is making clear that the tools to build a circular, on-demand fashion future already exist today.

sessions.title: Writing for humans in an AI world
sessions.description: Content designers at Anthropic don't write Claude's responses—they write everything around them: prompt starters, UI copy, feature names, voice principles. This talk explores how content design shifts when your interface understands natural language and how to teach collaboration (not just use) through the edges of the experience. Whether you're designing AI products or wondering how AI changes the craft, this talk addresses the challenge of designing frames around intelligence that talks back.

sessions.title: How to build content systems for AI product workflows
sessions.description: With AI, teams are shipping faster than ever. Designers are prototyping directly in the product. Engineers are coding at the speed of thought. But every tool your team touches is generating copy. Keeping it consistent, on-brand, and compliant—has never been harder. In this workshop, Jessica and Jolena, co-founders of Ditto, will show you how to build a content system that keeps up. Using Figma Make and Ditto, you’ll wire together a living system that generates copy from your guidelines, flags what doesn’t align, and gives every teammate—and every AI tool—the same source of copy truth.

sessions.title: Human methods, new material: designing with AI for the classroom
sessions.description: How do you build with AI for a world as human, messy, and high-stakes as the classroom? This talk takes you inside the 0–1 journey of Goodnotes Classroom: from real teacher workarounds and classroom observations to fast prototypes, co-creation sessions, and product decisions shaped by educators themselves. We’ll share how human methods became the foundation for designing with AI as a new material, and why the best way to build faster may be to get closer to the people you’re building for.

sessions.title: Skill issue: how we built an OS for PMs at Figma
sessions.description: This is the story of how a small group of volunteers, empowered by a culture of experimentation, created an AI tool that quickly became part of every PM's daily workflow. This talk is for product managers, designers, and anyone interested in shaping the tools that shape work itself.

sessions.title: ESPN's migration playbook: the move to Figma with One North
sessions.description: Each game day broadcast features a host of slick visuals – digital scoreboards, player stats, and matchup graphics. But behind the scenes, each individual design requires a huge lift to get from concept to live on air. In this session, One North and ESPN will share how migrating from Photoshop to Figma revealed new ways to collaborate, scale, and accelerate workflows. From initial adoption to transformative impact, this talk highlights not only how Figma helped push ESPN's design system to the next level, but how their design system pushed One North to up our game, too.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: unlocking speed and visual expression with [REDACTED]
sessions.description: Learn about new ways to bring visual expression, consistency, and speed into your design workflow with [REDACTED].

sessions.title: The metacognitive design loop
sessions.description: Learn how Epsilon's Product Design Team transformed front-end development by shifting the QA process to the very beginning of the design phase. By combining a Metacognitive Design Loop with Figma's Code Connect and MCP with their CORE UI component library, the team has revolutionized product delivery and achieved unprecedented development velocity—moving from design to production faster than ever before.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Opening keynote
sessions.description: Design and product-building are changing, and so are the roles we play in the process. This means rethinking product development from the ground up—shifting mindsets and methods to make great work. Figma’s biggest product launch of the year kicks off Config.

sessions.title: Be the director: how stage craft informs product craft
sessions.description: The screen is the stage, and the user is the audience. In this talk, we’ll explore how to think like a stage director by making intentional choices, defining the non-negotiables in your script, and finding your product’s spine. And why the show must go on.

sessions.title: Scooter, screen and duct-tape: from hack to habit
sessions.description: What if your next great design tool doesn’t exist yet? At Ather Energy, designers built their own using Arduinos, iPads, and Figma. This talk unpacks the ways of going outside the design loop, how hacks become habits, and how scrappy experimentation helps teams validate ideas, influence stakeholders, and build magical real-world experiences faster.

sessions.title: Figma meets hardware: prototyping for the physical world

speakers.name: Sam Thorne
speakers.title: Senior Principal Designer for Creative Technology

sessions.description: What if Figma’s canvas could extend into the physical world? At Electrolux, we use Figma to design the next generation of home appliances. This talk shares how we bridged the digital-physical gap by building "Figma Displays" with off-the-shelf electronics to live-stream prototypes onto production-accurate screens. Learn how this new process shrunk our feedback loop from six months to just two minutes, completely revamping our workflow. You'll leave inspired to find your own ways to connect the digital canvas to the world around you.

sessions.title: Keynote
sessions.description: Holly Herndon has spent the last decade building at the frontier of music and AI, from the albums Platform and PROTO to Holly+, the first artist-controlled voice model, to the data consent tools behind Spawning. This talk covers her approach to making art with AI, what she calls Protocol Art: a process-based practice of designing the rule set that creates the conditions for art to be made. Holly will discuss how the model-making process becomes a site of authorship, and where her studio with Mat Dryhurst is heading next: orchestrating agents while remaining at the conceptual center.

sessions.title: Beyond design systems: designing for robots and seniors
sessions.description: While AI reshapes the world, many who need it most are left behind. At Norbert Health, Anna Oh designs robotic AI for seniors with dementia and limited mobility in nursing homes. Working directly with real patients and nurses, Oh and her team built human systems that help robots listen, respond, and comfort.

sessions.title: From handoffs to upstream
sessions.description: A year ago, EDL made a bet. Not on a tool or a trend, but on a person and a different idea of what a design studio could own. Today, EDL pushes to clients' Git repos, runs their design systems in code, and sits upstream of their engineering teams. This wasn't accidental. It was intentional. This is the full story: the moment the team saw the opportunity, the rituals that made it real, the metrics that proved it worked, and what it means for any design leader wondering if their team could do the same.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: bringing designs to life with [REDACTED]
sessions.description: Ease into [REDACTED] in this session; we’ll show how to bring more energy into your product experience. Everything you need to know to get started with these new features.

sessions.title: From concept to confidence with Dscout AI
sessions.description: AI has accelerated the time between idea and execution. Teams can now generate concepts, prototypes, and products in days. Everyone’s a builder now. But speed without care, validation, and taste creates expensive mistakes. In this session, you’ll learn how Dscout’s AI-powered research suite supports the entire product development system—from ideation to iterative testing—while enabling designers, PMs, and product teams to make decisions quickly and confidently.

sessions.title: Designing the product that can do anything
sessions.description: Software, robotics, machine intelligence, and spatial computing have collided to present us with one of the greatest UX challenges of our time: How do you design an interface for a humanoid robot that can theoretically do anything? This talk will dive into the design challenges of teaching a robot about the world in three parts: Training robots to develop skills, building application interfaces to author task behaviors, and integrating with facilities to monitor robots’ effectiveness and realize the vision of the software-defined factory.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: The most important component: community
sessions.description: The more AI enters the creative process, the louder the question gets: what makes our work distinctly ours? This talk makes the case that community - real human connection, shared perspective, and creative kinship - is the answer. And why Figma is building tools that keep it at the center.

sessions.title: Prototypes, failures, and unexpected breakthroughs
sessions.description: Noah spends many days at Figma prototyping janky interactions, and other days perfecting editor microinteractions so subtle they almost go unnoticed. How do these two engineering mindsets interact? And how have they shaped the way Noah builds experiences like Figma Slides, FigPals, and interactive transit personality tests?

sessions.title: How structured thinking gives your AI superpowers
sessions.description: The best designers in the AI era will use it to scale their thinking, not just generate stuff. This talk looks at how a more structured design process (in docs, issues, trackers, and briefs) turns AI from a one-off creative aid into a genuine collaborator.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: design to development workflows
sessions.description: A beginner-friendly guide to moving from design to code using Figma’s MCP server. Learn best practices for bringing code context into Figma with variable prefixes and Code Connect, and how to get stronger results from your design system.

sessions.title: Your best practices are trapped in your head—and your AI has no idea
sessions.description: Your tone of voice, your component principles, your definition of "done"—this is the taste you've built over years. Right now it lives in your head, in scattered docs, or in places your tools can't see. Every AI session starts from scratch because that context is invisible. This talk shows how to connect those dots: building a shared knowledge layer so AI can help with specs, prototypes, and design systems—without you repeating yourself every time.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Ready for the world
sessions.description: Vicki Tan is a product designer who stumbled into authorship through Figma—a tool that gave her the courage to create an illustrated book about decision-making. This talk reveals the unexpected, messy journey of turning a movable concept into print, exploring thousands of design iterations, learning new skills in writing and drawing, and how that led to an untraditional memoir. By the end, she hopes you’ll be ready for the world, and your own project, knowing that belief in your tools and yourself is all you need to make it happen.

sessions.title: Hacking AI for creative operations at scale
sessions.description: Most creative teams are using AI to make assets faster. The real opportunity is further upstream—inside the systems, workflows, and decision logic that govern how creative gets made at scale. In this session, Rory Flynn shows how mastering three core skills—deconstruction, system prompts, and workflow building—unlocks the full power of Figma Weave for any team. You'll see what real deployment looks like from inside a billion-dollar global consumer tech brand, and what the future of creative ops looks like when AI is built in from the start.

sessions.title: Closing keynote
sessions.description: Want to animate like the pros and spend less time wrestling the graph editor? This session reveals a capture-to-curve workflow that turns recorded gestures into clean, editable timing you can drop into the tools you use. We will map a motion in the room to a UI element live and unpack a practical loop: capture, curve, polish, name. You will leave with a shared motion language you can reuse across design and code, plus techniques to make interfaces feel natural so newcomers move with confidence and experts move faster. We will demystify the curve editor and show how to ship feel, not graphs.

sessions.title: Out of sync, on purpose: desktop design after mobile won
sessions.description: This talk is about designing for Spotify’s desktop app—the original Spotify experience that continues to evolve alongside mobile. It explores how a small, focused team interprets features not by copying them 1:1, but by tailoring execution to the platform’s strengths. Desktop plays a distinct role in people’s routines—a companion for deep focus, creation, and immersive listening. Through real examples, Giulio shows how being small, deliberate, and a little out of sync can transform consistency into craft.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: the many ways to make with Figma Make
sessions.description: Learn about all of the different ways to build with Figma Make, whether you’re creating a new prototype from scratch or starting from an existing codebase. We’ll cover guidelines & skills, how to provide great context, and some best practices for working with your design system.

sessions.title: From kickoff to canvas: connecting Google Workspace and Figma
sessions.description: Stop wasting time hunting for project context across scattered tabs. This session explores the connected surface between Google Workspace and Figma. See a high-productivity workflow in action, from live ideation in Meet to using Ask Gemini in Chat to instantly surface files, wrap up comment threads, and remove the administrative friction from your day.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: upskill on skills and agentic workflows
sessions.description: Skills are how AI learns a domain: your research frameworks, handoff standards, and definitions of done, so every session starts informed, not from scratch. This session goes beyond the basics to show how to design, author, and deploy skills that make agents measurably faster and more precise for the work at hand. You’ll leave with a skill you helped build, not just a framework to complete later.

sessions.title: Closing keynote
sessions.description: André Anjos is a musician, not a designer, but he believes everyone is facing the same shift. Average has become free. Machines can now produce competent, good-enough work instantly, whether it’s a song or a screen. That means the only work worth doing is the work only you can make. After twenty years in music, he has learned one enduring truth: the work you create for yourself is the work that ultimately reaches everyone else. There has never been a better time to have a voice.

sessions.title: Own the loop: how designers and researchers win with AI evals
sessions.description: AI is everywhere, but how do we know if it's actually good? The teams winning with AI aren't just moving fast, they're measuring well. And that measurement starts with a human-centered definition of "good" that lives in design and research. AI evals have long been an engineering problem. That's changing. As capabilities commoditize, design is the new moat. This talk is an invitation for designers and researchers to lead and become architects of how we evaluate AI against real human needs.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: Managing multiple creative passions in a world that tells you to specialize
sessions.description: Lauren will share how she’s managed multiple creative passions, throughout her 15-year career, in a world that tells you to specialize.

sessions.title: The canvas is not dead
sessions.description: You've probably heard it: 'the canvas is dead. Just prompt your way to a UI and ship it.' But Catt doesn't work on simple problems. She designs for tens of millions of users, inside products with years of context and use cases AI tools can't easily handle. When the complexity is that deep, you can't skip the part where you actually think. She'll walk through her framework for deciding what deserves deep, by-hand exploration versus what to hand off to the machine. You'll leave knowing how to match your tools to your actual problems.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive
sessions.description: Take a deep dive into Figma's latest products. Details will be revealed during our opening keynote product launch.

sessions.title: In praise of dark mode
sessions.description: Good design emits as little light as possible. This talk makes the case for Dark Mode Maximalism — that all user interfaces should be designed in dark mode. Drawing on two decades of design/engineering practice and arcane scholarship, Baytaş explains that dark mode is not a toggle, but the default condition of good design.

sessions.title: Designing in the age of the 10x engineer
sessions.description: Andy Zhang will explore how the designer's role evolves when creation becomes frictionless. He’ll look at the future of dynamic, intent-driven experiences and discuss the questions we must ask to adapt our product processes—ensuring that faster engineering leads to better, human-centered products rather than just more features.

sessions.title: Don't forget the feeling: a critique of software in 2026
sessions.description: What does making feel like in 2026? Luis will explore how he learned to stop worrying and love AI, or at least stop rejecting it to accelerate his creative output. Through critique of popular AI tools, Luis will explain that in this fast paced environment it's essential that we don't forget that feeling and brand are what brings users back.

sessions.title: Usernames on Instagram: how a craft fix became an identity crisis
sessions.description: This talk traces that spiral—from @dril to Baudrillard, from the pumpkin spice latte to the phone icon that still looks like a landline—and arrives somewhere concrete: In the Internet Age, users don't just adapt to what we build, they create something new with it. Representations drift from reality, and people don’t just adapt to products—they complete them. The result is a firsthand case study in product judgment when human behavior becomes the strongest constraint, why designing with curiosity and far-fetched futurism matters even more in fast-moving environments.

sessions.title: Dense by design
sessions.description: Why did Google beat Yahoo? Why does Bloomberg Terminal still dominate? It's not about cramming more onto the screen—it's about maximizing value in time and space. This talk unpacks UI density from first principles, moving through Tufte, gestalt psychology, and perceived performance to arrive at a framework you can actually use.

sessions.title: Opening keynotes
sessions.description: This talk explores human computer interaction through the design of programmable creative tools. Drawing on our work maintaining Strudel, a live-coded music system, we’ll examine how having absolute freedom over text interfaces enables more intimate and natural interactions. With a background in instrument making, we frame software as an interactive artifact meant to be tuned and learned through use engaging with a design space that hasn’t fully crystallized yet, where shaping your own environment becomes a playground for creativity.

sessions.title: 10 years of daily sketching
sessions.description: Ten years ago, after a rough year, Zach Lieberman posted a small code sketch on Instagram on New Year's Day and he's posted one every day since. This talk is about what he's learned from that practice of making something every day. He'll focus on the importance of boredom, how to structure iteration, how he structures research and most importantly, finding creative freedom.

sessions.title: The marathon designer: creative endurance beyond prompts and pixels
sessions.description: In a world obsessed with speed & chaos, design’s biggest threat isn’t AI—it’s burnout, pixel-pushing, doing things the old way, waiting for permission. This talk introduces a framework for creative endurance beyond prompts and pixels. Through three core practices—sustainable pacing, builder culture, and versatile hiring—Socrates Charisis shares lessons from running a bootstrapped studio in Athens. Expect real stories, actionable systems, and new ways to keep creativity alive when the work gets dull and the tools keep changing.

sessions.title: Closing happy hour
sessions.description: Toast to new connections and inspirational sessions at Config Commons!

sessions.title: The future is low-tech: lessons from the early 2000s
sessions.description: We’ll rewind to the early 2000s—when tech was low-tech, playful and non-extractive. Think: loading bars, Tamagotchis and RollerCoaster Tycoon in a cereal box. This talk explores calm, nervous-system-friendly design: attention regulation, slowness as a feature, autonomy-first mechanics, real-world interaction and gentle feedback loops. A call for designers and builders to make tech feel human again.

sessions.title: Figma deep dive: inside the Weave workflow
sessions.description: Dive into how Weave creative work actually gets made—not the polished final frame, but the decisions behind it. Explore branching, sequencing, iteration, model chaining, edits, mistakes, recoveries, and the systems thinking that turn AI from a magic trick into a real creative process.

sessions.title: Quirk up your workplace
sessions.description: This talk is about encouraging people to be a little more silly, a little more playful when you’re at work. The rituals, gimmicks, and stunts we’ve done at GitHub to make remote work fun and interesting.

sessions.title: The hidden craft of interface design in modern film
sessions.description: Step behind the screens of modern cinema. Jayse Hansen takes you from sketchbook to IMAX, revealing the story-driven way holograms, HUDs, and futuristic interface systems are designed, animated, and brought to life for blockbuster films. Spanning more than 20 major franchises, from Iron Man and The Hunger Games to Tron and Star Wars, this is a rare backstage look at the meticulously detailed craft of designing technology that doesn’t exist, but feels like it should.

sessions.title: Everyone's still eating: building Notion's design system
sessions.description: Introducing a design system at a design-led company can be like trying to whip a tablecloth off a beautifully set table while people are still eating—and maybe put a nicer tablecloth on at the same time. As part of Notion’s newly formed design system team (est. 2025), we’ll share the tools and techniques we’re using to refactor Notion’s visual foundations and improve things from the inside, without slowing down our peers or compromising the spirit of thoughtful, handcrafted care that makes Notion feel like Notion.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: express yourself with Figma Draw
sessions.description: A hands-on collaborative session where attendees come together to create a shared art piece. This session celebrates the magic of expressive design and showcases what’s possible with Figma Draw.

sessions.title: Designing money: how to build a trustworthy brand in the age of AI
sessions.description: How do we pair machine learning with true artistry? What’s moral, what’s safe, and what genuinely resonates in this new creative world? These are the questions that confronted our team at Plaid as we sought to reshape the way money looks and feels in the age of AI. In this far-reaching talk, we’ll explore the ways authenticity has shaped the history of finance through the lens of graphic design and branding—and what machine learning means for its future.

sessions.title: Design systems anarchy
sessions.description: Most people don't get into design systems to be a cop. But we've become exactly that. Product teams go around us while we create rules nobody follows. Punk figured this out decades ago. You need shared ethos, basic infrastructure, and people who care. Quality comes from culture, not compliance. Drawing from years running an indie record label and nearly 10 years leading systems at scale at Block, Dropbox, and Expedia, this talk applies punk's blueprint to systems. AI handed designers the means of production. The gatekeeping model is dead. Stop managing systems. Start nurturing subcultures.

sessions.title: Designing brand intelligence: from rules to expression
sessions.description: In an AI-powered design world, the designer’s role is fundamentally shifting. The real edge is no longer technical execution but how you think, decide, and design in a world changing faster than your design system. This talk reimagines brand guides as cognitive frameworks inspired by J Dilla, who reimagined rhythm and broke away from rigid quantization to create something more human, more fluid, more felt.

sessions.title: Legibility by design
sessions.description: Building trust in AI isn't about transparency; it’s about communication design. Whether it’s an autonomous vehicle or a generative agent, the challenge is the same: how much should a system communicate? Drawing from years at Waymo, this session explores a framework for trust. We’ll move beyond automation toward legibility, where users understand intent rather than just monitoring tasks. Learn to calibrate feedback to avoid alert fatigue and build AI products that feel predictable and fundamentally trustworthy.

sessions.title: AI will upend your process—for the better
sessions.description: AI gives us an opportunity to rethink how work gets done across the software delivery lifecycle. In this talk, I’ll show concrete examples of how AI removes bottlenecks that keep us from discovering and delivering value. We’ll explore how AI supports building design systems, generates implementation‑ready user stories, and writes production code directly from Figma assets. The result: faster validation, earlier feedback, and more time for craft.

sessions.title: Gnarly by design: building systems with grit
sessions.description: Clean design systems are hard to create, but gritty, texture-heavy design systems are even harder. Join Beau for a journey into a video game design system and learn how to add some character to your squeaky-clean system and make it scaleable.

sessions.title: Designed to be read: making a system AI can actually use
sessions.description: Hear the story of how Meta worked directly with Figma to test design systems in Figma Make. You’ll learn how, in our quest to push the boundaries of AI-driven quality, Meta’s business design system team was inspired to rebuild their legacy design system for business tools from the ground up. Our talk covers the “sloppy” challenges we encountered, how we resolved them in close partnership with Figma, and outlines a 3-step repeatable playbook that any team can use to deliver high-quality AI-generated designs—even without rebuilding your entire design system.

sessions.title: The invisible mechanisms in your interfaces
sessions.description: For 15 years, Johannes has been taking interfaces apart and collecting the mechanisms underneath. There’s a name for when your cursor sits between bold and regular and the system can’t tell you which wins. It’s called cursor affinity. Your tools are full of mechanisms like this — each with an inventor, a constraint, and a lineage you can trace. This talk takes them apart, names them, and hands you a practice for disassembling any interface you touch. You’ll leave with the words to make others see what you see.

sessions.title: Preparing Rocket for the AI era
sessions.description: While many companies are still preparing for AI, Rocket is already embedding it into the fabric of how thousands of team members work every day. Learn how we're leveraging Figma's ecosystem—design systems, Code Connect, Figma Make, and the Figma MCP—to power AI-native workflows at scale and help more people achieve their dream of homeownership.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: workflow tips—from slow to flow
sessions.description: In this session we’ll share a collection of helpful tips and workflow tricks that can help you work smarter, and faster on the canvas and beyond.

sessions.title: The world of street art and care-filled design
sessions.description: After studying art and design at UC Berkeley, Ella Rochelle-Lawton ran off to join the circus and designed a billboard about it. Since then, teaching street art history and spray paint has taught her that the best design lives on the street, where people can touch it and make it their own. In this talk, she'll share how working with teams from Adobe, Google, and Figma transformed her view of design from pixels to people. She’ll explore how street art, from the local legends to international artists, build belonging, redefine public space, and inspire community throughout the design process.

sessions.title: Design for the future of apps
sessions.description: Generative AI is not only about text chats and image generation. At Google, AI is also increasingly used to compose ephemeral UI on demand, a pattern called Generative UI. While this promises unparalleled personalization for each user, it also risks regressions in UX pillars like consistency and clear information hierarchy. Learn how designers can adapt design systems to realize the full potential GenUI while also mitigating its risks.

sessions.title: Building real-time user insights into your design workflow
sessions.description: Most design teams ship, then learn. You don't have to do that anymore. In this session, HeyMarvin CEO Prayag Narula walks through what it takes to build real-time user feedback into your designs. You'll hear about the integrations and tools that make it work in practice and what it looks like when research finally shows up where decisions get made. If you've ever shipped something and wished you'd heard from a user two weeks earlier, this one's for you.

sessions.title: 300GB story: how to inspire action when data isn’t enough
sessions.description: What if 300 GB of climate data could make you feel something? In this talk, two Harvard Design Engineering students share how they turned overwhelming datasets into Habitat2100, an interactive storytelling experience built in Figma that transforms data into empathy, urgency, and a call to act for our planet’s future.

sessions.title: Figma learning lab: build your first Weave workflow
sessions.description: Node-based tools look technical until you build something with them. In this hands-on session, you'll go from blank canvas to working workflow, connecting models, directing outputs, and seeing exactly how Figma Weave gives you control over every step.

sessions.title: Designing with confidence in an AI-powered world
sessions.description: AI has fundamentally changed the pace of design. Ideas move faster. Iterations are instant. Options are endless. But speed introduces a new tension: decisions that are harder to defend. In this session, Bobby Meixner shares findings from UserTesting’s latest research on defensible design in the age of AI and explores how leading teams are closing the confidence gap. You’ll see how embedding real human insight directly inside the design workflow allows teams to move quickly, without sacrificing certainty. Because in an AI-powered world, the real advantage isn’t speed. It’s conviction.
